qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated payment editor overwrite leads to arbitrary PHP execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: It identifies the same unauthenticated `/payment/api/editable/update` route, the same `ApiController::file_put` overwrite of published PHP payment hook files, and the same later `PaymentService::convertKeyToModel()` `require` path described by the advisory.

selected_finding.title: Unauthenticated payment editor overwrite leads to arbitrary PHP execution

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only qualifying finding matches the advisory’s root cause exactly: an unauthenticated `/payment/api/editable/update` route writes attacker-controlled PHP into published payment hook files via `file_put_contents()`, and later payment processing loads those files with `require()`. A copied PoC run under `.judge` then demonstrated attacker-controlled PHP execution through the documented `/pay/click/1/1000` trigger path.
evidence: At commit `2c5fa0515bdb01b403bf3cacf419afca2738d05b`, `src/routes/web.php` places `/payment/api/editable/update` under middleware derived from `config/config.php`, whose default `control_panel.middleware` is `null` and therefore falls back to `['web']` rather than authentication middleware.

`src/Http/Controllers/ApiController.php` writes attacker-controlled `content` into `base_path('/app/Http/Controllers/Payments/'.$request['file_name'].'.php')` with `file_put_contents()` when the target file exists.

`src/Services/PaymentService.php` later `require`s `app/Http/Controllers/Payments/key_model.php`, and the package README documents `/pay/{paysys}/{key}/{amount}` calling `PaymentService::convertKeyToModel($key)`.

The copied PoC run in `.judge/poc_runs/unauthenticated_payment_editor_rce` logged `EVOHUNT-491ec345-ARBITRARY-PHP` from `/tmp/evohunt-host/storage/app/evohunt-proof.txt` and ended with `SUCCESS: attacker-controlled PHP executed through /pay/click/1/1000 after unauthenticated editor access`.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC provisions a Laravel host app, publishes the package’s editable payment files, overwrites `app/Http/Controllers/Payments/key_model.php` through `/payment/api/editable/update`, then requests `/pay/click/1/1000`. `run.log` shows `/tmp/evohunt-host/storage/app/evohunt-proof.txt` containing `EVOHUNT-491ec345-ARBITRARY-PHP`, proving attacker-controlled PHP executed in the target application context.
